response: {"name":"eCLIP-seq data processing","description":"Computational pipeline for processing eCLIP-seq sequencing data from raw reads through demultiplexing, adapter trimming, genome alignment, PCR duplicate removal, and peak calling to identify RNA-binding protein binding sites.","data_type":"sequencing","raw_data_source":"GEO: GSE78487","steps":[{"step_number":1,"description":"Standard HiSeq demultiplexing followed by a second demultiplexing step using custom scripts for libraries with distinct inline barcodes; the random-mer (UMI) is appended to the read name for later use.","input_data":"Raw FASTQ reads from HiSeq sequencing with inline barcodes","output_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ reads with random-mer appended to read names","software":"Custom scripts (Illumina HiSeq demultiplexing + custom inline barcode demultiplexing)","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":2,"description":"Adapter trimming of demultiplexed reads; reads shorter than 18 bp after trimming are discarded.","input_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ reads","output_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ reads (≥18 bp)","software":"cutadapt","software_version":"1.9.dev1","parameters":{"min_length":"18"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":3,"description":"Mapping trimmed reads against human repetitive elements in RepBase to segregate repeat-mapping reads for separate analysis.","input_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ reads","output_data":"Repeat-mapped reads (segregated) and unmapped reads for genomic alignment","software":"STAR","software_version":"2.4.0i","parameters":{"reference":"RepBase v18.05 (human elements)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":4,"description":"Mapping of non-repeat reads against the full human genome (hg19) including a splice junction database to produce uniquely mapping aligned reads.","input_data":"Non-repeat FASTQ reads from Step 3","output_data":"Uniquely mapped aligned reads (BAM files)","software":"STAR","software_version":"2.4.0i","parameters":{"genome":"hg19","annotation":"splice junction database"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":5,"description":"PCR duplicate removal using a custom script that identifies duplicates based on identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer (UMI) sequence, producing 'Usable' reads.","input_data":"Uniquely mapped aligned reads (BAM files)","output_data":"PCR-deduplicated 'Usable' reads (BAM files)","software":"Custom PCR duplicate removal script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"dedup_criteria":"Read1 start position + Read2 start position + random-mer sequence"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":6,"description":"For eCLIP datasets with multiple inline barcodes, usable reads from individual barcodes are merged into a single dataset at the usable-read stage prior to peak calling.","input_data":"Usable reads from individual inline barcode libraries (BAM files)","output_data":"Merged usable reads BAM file","software":null,"software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":7,"description":"Cluster (peak) identification on usable reads using CLIPper, considering Read 2 only (enriched for crosslink-site terminations), with binomial threshold method and Bonferroni correction.","input_data":"Merged usable reads (BAM files)","output_data":"Called peak/cluster BED files","software":"CLIPper","software_version":"1.0","parameters":{"species":"hg19","options":"-o --bonferroni --superlocal --threshold-method binomial --save-pickle","read_used":"Read 2 only"},"code_reference":"https://github.com/YeoLab/clipper/releases/tag/1.0"},{"step_number":8,"description":"RPM normalization of read tracks against the total number of usable reads in each dataset for visualization on the UCSC Genome Browser.","input_data":"Usable reads (BAM files)","output_data":"RPM-normalized genome browser tracks","software":"UCSC Genome Browser","software_version":null,"parameters":{"normalization":"RPM (reads per million usable reads)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":9,"description":"Downsampling analysis by 100 iterations of randomly permuting uniquely mapped reads, selecting the top N reads, and performing PCR duplicate removal to derive usable read counts at varying depths.","input_data":"Uniquely mapped reads (BAM files)","output_data":"Usable read counts at varying sequencing depths (downsampling curves)","software":"Custom scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"iterations":"100","method":"random permutation + top-N selection + PCR duplicate removal"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":10,"description":"For iCLIP experiments with multiple cDNA-size libraries (low, medium, high) per sample, only the library with the highest percent usable reads is selected for downsampling analysis.","input_data":"Usable reads from multiple cDNA-size iCLIP libraries","output_data":"Selected single best library per sample for downsampling","software":null,"software_version":null,"parameters":{"selection_criterion":"highest percent usable reads"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":11,"description":"De novo motif finding for RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP clusters using HOMER's findMotifs program, comparing cluster sequences against background regions of the same genic region type.","input_data":"CLIPper-called cluster sequences (FASTA) and background same-genic-region random sequences","output_data":"Enriched RNA motifs","software":"HOMER findMotifs","software_version":null,"parameters":{"threads":"4","rna":"true","motif_count":"10","motif_lengths":"5,6,7,8,9","background":"3 random same-sized same-genic-region regions per cluster"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":12,"description":"Cluster location annotation by counting total bases covered by peaks for each genic region type (introns, 3′UTRs, etc.) to generate cluster location pie charts.","input_data":"CLIPper-called peak BED files","output_data":"Per-genic-region peak coverage counts for pie charts","software":"Custom scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"metric":"total number of bases covered per region type"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":13,"description":"For motif enrichment analysis before and after SMInput normalization, CLIPper-identified clusters are extended to a minimum of 100 nt centered around the cluster midpoint.","input_data":"CLIPper-called cluster BED files","output_data":"Extended cluster regions (≥100 nt, centered on midpoint)","software":"Custom scripts","software_version":null,"parameters":{"min_width":"100 nt","centering":"midpoint of CLIPper-identified cluster"},"code_reference":null}],"figures_produced":["Figure 1","Figure 1a","Figure 1b","Figure 1b–c","Figure 1d","Figure 2","Figure 2a","Figure 2b","Figure 2c","Figure 2d","Figure
